summary: The test run crashed with a segmentation fault while executing numpy.argsort from a datetime test (test_datetime_nat_argsort_stability). The Python process terminated abnormally (exit code 245).
root_cause: Process terminated by a segmentation fault triggered during numpy._core.fromnumeric.argsort called from test_datetime_nat_argsort_stability.

evidence.reason: Shows the process crashed with a segmentation fault.
Stack frame indicates execution was in numpy._core.fromnumeric._wrapfunc when the crash occurred.
Stack frame shows the crash occurred while calling argsort.
Identifies the specific test that invoked argsort and led to the crash.
CI job terminated with a non-zero exit code immediately after the segmentation fault.
The npysort x86-simd-sort code (a C extension/simd-backed sort) is present in the build and may be implicated in an argsort crash.

suggested_fix: Reproduce the crash locally by running the failing test in isolation and capture a native backtrace (e.g. python -m pytest numpy/_core/tests/test_datetime.py::test_datetime_nat_argsort_stability -q). Build NumPy with debug symbols and/or address sanitizer (or run under gdb/valgrind) to get a C-level stack trace and inspect npysort/argsort implementation (e.g. rebuild via meson/ninja with debug/ASAN enabled) to identify the faulty C extension code.
